title: Poupartia chapelieri
taxonomy: Poupartia chapelieri belongs to the family Anacardiaceae, which includes other plants like cashew and poison ivy. This family is commonly known as the cashew or sumac family. The genus Poupartia is native to Madagascar and is closely related to the genus Toxicodendron. Poupartia chapelieri is a species of tree that is endemic to the island of Madagascar. It is a part of the flowering plant kingdom, specifically within the angiosperms.
